attributes.description: Display label of the stage (e.g., "Qualification," "Closed Won").
attributes.name: stageName
attributes.usage: Drives kanban column headers, funnel chart labels, and pipeline- review conversation ("the deal is in Negotiation"). Should be short and verb-phrase or noun-phrase that captures the stage meaning.

attributes.description: Machine-readable identifier for the stage (used by integrations and automation rules).
attributes.name: apiName
attributes.usage: Distinct from the display name to allow display-name editing without breaking integrations. HubSpot stage IDs are GUIDs; Salesforce uses MasterLabel + ApiName pair; EspoCRM uses the enum value directly.

attributes.description: Default likelihood of closing for opportunities at this stage, expressed as a percentage.
attributes.name: probability
attributes.usage: Auto-applied to opportunities when they enter the stage (unless manually overridden). Drives weighted-pipeline calculations. Salesforce and HubSpot both support per-stage probability; EspoCRM derives it from the stage; Attio and CiviCRM don't have probability tracking.

attributes.description: Numeric sort position of the stage within its pipeline.
attributes.name: order
attributes.usage: Drives the left-to-right order of kanban board columns and the progression order in funnel reports. Editable by admins via drag- to-reorder UI in most systems.

attributes.description: Reference to the parent pipeline this stage belongs to (when multiple pipelines exist).
attributes.name: pipeline
attributes.usage: In multi-pipeline configurations, stages belong to one pipeline. HubSpot's Pipeline entity is explicit; Salesforce uses Sales Processes (RecordType-pipeline mappings) rather than a separate Pipeline entity.

attributes.description: Classification of stage for forecasting purposes (which forecast bucket opportunities at this stage belong to).
attributes.name: forecastCategory
attributes.usage: Salesforce-specific concept that maps stages to forecast categories for sales forecasting. Allows reps to commit deals at certain stages without having them all roll up the same way. Less common in other systems.

attributes.description: Flag indicating whether opportunities in this stage are considered closed (won or lost).
attributes.name: isClosed
attributes.usage: Distinguishes open-pipeline stages from terminal stages. Drives "open opportunities" filtering and pipeline aging reports (don't count closed opps as "stale"). Salesforce uses IsClosed; HubSpot uses dealstage probability=100 (won) or 0 (lost); Attio uses a stage type.

attributes.description: Flag indicating whether opportunities in this stage are considered won.
attributes.name: isWon
attributes.usage: Distinguishes Closed Won from Closed Lost. Critical for win-rate calculations. Salesforce uses IsWon explicitly; HubSpot uses probability=100 to indicate won. Attio uses stage classification.

attributes.description: Display color of the stage (typically a hex code).
attributes.name: color
attributes.usage: Drives kanban-board column tinting and funnel-chart segment colors. Pure cosmetic. HubSpot, Attio, and EspoCRM support stage colors; Salesforce does not.

attributes.description: Flag indicating whether the stage is available for use.
attributes.name: isActive
attributes.usage: Lets admins retire stages without deleting them (preserving historical opportunities that were in that stage). Inactive stages hide from new-opportunity creation but remain in reports for historical records.

business_context: Pipeline Stage records control the columns of every kanban board, the buckets of every funnel report, and the conversion-rate metrics in every pipeline review. Pipeline Stages are typically modeled in one of three ways: (1) configuration entities — separate records the admin manages via a UI, with admin operations like add/rename/delete/reorder (HubSpot, Attio); (2) picklist values on the parent entity — defined inline as enum values on Opportunity.stage with no separate Stage table (Salesforce uses OpportunityStage as a setup record but the picklist values on Opportunity.StageName are what matter operationally; EspoCRM uses a true enum); (3) custom field values — pipeline behavior achieved by repurposing a status discriminator (CiviCRM contribution_status, Bloomerang transaction status). HubSpot has the most explicit Pipeline-as-a-thing model, with the Pipelines API allowing programmatic creation and management of pipelines and their stages. Salesforce treats pipeline stages as picklist values that can be configured with per-stage probability and "type" (Open / Closed Won / Closed Lost). When interviewing clients, surface "Pipeline Stage" by asking what stages records progress through and whether different teams or business units need distinct pipelines: a single shared pipeline (everyone uses the same stages) versus multi-pipeline configurations (separate pipelines for new business / renewals / channel sales / each business unit). Multi-pipeline support is unevenly available across systems and significantly affects deployment complexity.
name: Pipeline Stage
purpose: A discrete step in a pipeline (sales pipeline, support-ticket pipeline, lead-qualification pipeline, donation-cultivation pipeline, etc.) that records can move through. Each stage typically has a name, an order, and a probability or status classification.

relationships.description: A stage classifies many opportunities; each opportunity has one current stage.
Stages belong to a parent Pipeline (in multi-pipeline configurations).

sources.title: HubSpot: Set up and manage object pipelines
Attio: Create and manage kanban views (stage-based)

systems.name: Status attribute on Deals
systems.notes: Attio represents stages as values on a Status attribute on the Deals object. Stages are configured via the workspace settings UI; the attribute drives kanban-board column configuration. Each stage has a label and a color but no separate probability concept — Attio doesn't compute weighted-pipeline values out of the box.

systems.name: Status (on Transactions)
systems.notes: Bloomerang doesn't have a sales-pipeline concept. Transaction status is a discriminator (Completed, Pledged, Failed) but not a pipeline stage in the cultivation-progression sense.

systems.name: contribution_status (option group)
systems.notes: CiviCRM doesn't have a sales-pipeline concept. The closest analog is contribution_status_id on Contribution records (Completed, Pending, Cancelled, Failed, Refunded, In Progress). This serves a status role rather than a pipeline-stage role since contributions don't typically progress through cultivation stages.

systems.name: stage (enum on Opportunity)
systems.notes: EspoCRM models stages as enum values directly on Opportunity.stage. No separate Stage entity — admin configuration happens via the Entity Manager's enum options editor. Per-stage probability is configured via opportunity.probability auto-population logic but isn't a separate stored mapping table.

systems.name: Pipeline + Deal Stage
systems.notes: HubSpot has the most explicit Pipeline-as-an-entity model. Pipelines and Deal Stages are managed via the Pipelines API. Each pipeline has stages with internal names (machine-readable IDs) and labels (display names), plus per-stage probability and "ticket state" classifications. Multiple pipelines per object are supported (Sales Hub Starter+ for deals; Service Hub for tickets).

systems.name: OpportunityStage (setup) / StageName (picklist)
systems.notes: Salesforce models stages as a setup-level entity (OpportunityStage) with attributes like MasterLabel, ApiName, IsActive, IsClosed, IsWon, ForecastCategory, DefaultProbability, SortOrder. The values surface as a picklist on Opportunity.StageName. Multiple Sales Processes can be defined to support different stage sets per Record Type.

systems.name: OpportunityStage (with NPSP-specific stages per Donation Record Type)
systems.notes: Same Salesforce OpportunityStage. NPSP commonly configures donation-specific stages: Prospecting, Cultivation, Solicitation, Negotiation, Pledged, Posted (Closed Won), Closed Lost. Sales Processes are commonly used to give different stage sets to Donation, Major Gift, Grant, and Recurring Donation Record Types.
